method: web.run evidence synthesis; claims require citations in chat final

sources.claim: Attractive interfaces can increase perceived usability and tolerance for minor issues, but can also mask UX problems.
Visual design should communicate and support function; clarity beats decorative excess.
Visual design should be tested with methods such as 5-second, first-click, and preference tests.
WCAG 2.2 adds focus visibility, target size, dragging alternatives, consistent help, and related criteria relevant to interactive HTML.
Material Design 3 provides a current open design system for color, typography, layout, components, and accessibility.
Typography decisions must support visual accessibility, including contrast between text and background.
Expressive design uses color, shape, size, motion, and containment to communicate function and emotion.

synthesis: Visual quality is not a cosmetic afterthought; it affects trust, perceived usability, willingness to continue, and adult credibility judgments.

For MetaBlooms HTML activities, the correct gate is cross-cutting: MPP must require a visual contract for visual artifacts, while each artifact cartridge must instantiate a purpose-specific design profile.
The gate must block default-browser styling, unbounded animations, inaccessible contrast/focus/touch targets, and visual choices unsupported by purpose/audience/platform.
The gate must require both visual intent and verification evidence, not just a note saying the artifact looks good.
